title: the Coinage of Two Hundred Rupees and Ten Rupees Coins to commemorate the occasion of 200' H BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF TATYA TOPE Rules, 2015.

MT-zi d% .(-1 (-Ctft. 2.5 cri-u-a- oil s W{ Zfl- EffT W4- ( -urd) c:I ei ei 4.45 TIM- 3.11-T g:Tt-zi 3.I-PT 3.26 ( Q eta P el 4-1 chl+11) Q (-1 P P ei di - 5.5 3 Cl-fra- # 6 31-2-tra" ch.Ttai # Ji-TT) 7.52 cuidi # 7.90 CI-IFTff, - 1.75 cifi-a- # 2 Cl-i -Wa. 31"-{ atar t- 1 d,I I di wr 3t-a-{ ,t-i chci 1 t- I Trtzr arm- tail f---J) d i 01 AT 1 i;ricur 0-4 --4- ZiT tl CI ch .( 3T2tra -- 3 of 6 -- THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] cl I # 74 1;rita. 76 crfua- AT' at 24 26 cifUf f 3taT [ i] Li ch 771T, aTq'T Trfqq CCR4.111.714 MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Economic Affairs ) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 23rd October, 2015 GS.R. 81 6(E).—In exercise of the powers conferred by clauses (d) and (e) of sub-section (2) of section 24 of the Coinage Act, 2011(11 of 2011), the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely: - 1. Short title and commencement. — (1) These rules may be called the Coinage of Two Hundred Rupees and Ten Rupees Coins to commemorate the occasion of 200' H BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF TATYA TOPE Rules, 2015. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. (2) Denomination of coins.-The coins of the following denominations shall also be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of "200TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF TATYA TOPE", namely:- Two Hundred Rupees; Ten Rupees. (3) Dimension and composition of coins. - The coins of the denominations mentioned in rule 2 shall conform to the following dimensions and composition, namely: - Denomination of coin Shape and outside diameter Number of serrations Metal composition (1) (2) (3) (4) Two Hundred Rupees Circular 44 millimeters 200 Quaternary alloy Silver - 50 per cent. Copper -40 per cent. Nickel - 05 per cent. Zinc - 05 per cent. Ten Rupees Circular 27 millimeters (Bi — Metallic) --- Outer ring (Aluminium Bronze) Copper -92 per cent. Aluminium - 06 per cent. Nickel - 02 per cent. Center piece (Cupro Nickel) Copper -75 per cent. Nickel - 25 per cent. -- 4 of 6 -- [ 1-117 3(i)] 41T7 : 3-T14T917T 5 (4) Design.- The design of the coins shall be as under: - TWO HUNDRED RUPEES OBVERSE: The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the center with the legend oiticr inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word "arRa" in Devnagari script and on the right periphery flanked with the word "INDIA" 'in English and it shall also bear the Rupee symbol "Z" and denominational value "200" in the international numerals below the Lion Capitol. REVERSE: The face of coin shall bear the portrait of "TATYA TOPE" in the center, with inscription "dlrell ZIA" A' 200 .11 4.1. cn" in Devnagari script on upper periphery and "200TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF TATYA TOPE" in English on the lower periphery. The year figure "2015" shall be written below the portrait in international numerals. TEN RUPEES OBVERSE: The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the center with the legend vi a" inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word " grid" in Devnagari script and on the right periphery flanked with the word "INDIA" in English and it shall also bear the Rupee symbol "Z" and denominational value "10" in the international numerals below the Lion Capitol. REVERSE: The face of coin shall bear the portrait of "TATYA TOPE" in the center, with inscription "curet! '64 *t 2004T oi ticft" in Devnagari script on upper periphery and "200TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF TATYA TOPE" in English on the lower periphery. The year figure "2015" shall be written below the portrait in international numerals. -- 5 of 6 -- _•2 6 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (5) Standard weight and remedy allowed.- The standard weight of the coins of the following denominations, coined to commemorate the occasion of "200TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF TATYA TOPE" and the remedy allowed in making of such coins shall be as specified in the Table below: - TABLE Denomination of coin Standard weight Remedy allowed In composition • In standard weight (1) (2) (3) (4) Two Hundred Rupees 35 Grams 1/500th plus or minus for Silver, that is to say the Silver content may vary from 498 to 502 per 1000. 1/100th plus or minus, that is to say, the weight could vary from 34.65 grams to 35.35 grams. Ten Rupees (Bi-Metallic) 7.71 Grams (Outer Ring 4.45 grams and center piece 3.26 grams) Outer Ring (Aluminium — Bronze) Aluminium 5.5 per cent to 6 per cent, Nickel — 1.75 per cent to 2 pe rcent and remainder are . copper. copp Center piece (Cupro —Nickel) plus or minus 1 percent for both copper and Nickel, that is to say, the copper may vary from 74-per cent to 76 percent and Nickel from 24 percent to 26 percent. Plus or minus 2.5 per cent, that is to say, the weight could vary from 7.52 grams to 7.90 grams. [F. No. 02/24/2015-Coin ] PANKAJ GUPTA, Under Secy.
